executive_brief:
The threat landscape has fundamentally shifted from human-paced campaigns to machine-speed exploitation. Artificial intelligence has compressed the vulnerability-to-exploitation timeline from weeks to hours, rendering traditional 30-to-90-day patching SLAs obsolete. CERT-In's emergency mandate requiring containment of internet-facing flaws within 12 hours [46, 112, 194] is not bureaucratic theater; it is a direct response to AI-driven reconnaissance and automated exploit generation that now weaponize zero-days before defenders finish triage. Simultaneously, Anthropic's Project Glasswing identified over 10,000 vulnerabilities in a single month [11, 47, 79], proving that AI accelerates discovery far beyond human remediation capacity. The patching backlog is now an active attack surface.

Identity controls are undergoing a parallel collapse. Multi-factor authentication is systematically bypassed by live-proxy PhaaS platforms and OAuth device code abuse, which intercept OTPs in real-time and harvest refresh tokens without passwords [7, 26, 43, 84, 192]. Extortion operators have abandoned purely digital chains for physical infiltration, deploying operatives to pose as IT support and drop malicious USB drives directly into corporate offices [88, 109, 134, 172]. The software supply chain is experiencing industrialized poisoning, with attackers rewriting Git tags, trojanizing CI/CD workflows, and compromising developer extensions at scale [12, 35, 58, 65, 181]. Defenders are facing a coordinated assault on the very tools they use to build, secure, and verify their environments.

The era of reactive security is over. Organizations must treat unpatched internet-facing assets as already compromised, enforce phishing-resistant authentication immediately, and implement continuous exposure management. The window for traditional defense has closed.

threat_stories.headline: The 12-Hour Clock: AI Compresses the Exploitation Window to Zero
threat_stories.narrative:
Artificial intelligence has shattered the traditional vulnerability management lifecycle. Threat actors now deploy LLMs to automate reconnaissance, generate functional exploits, and orchestrate mass deployment, compressing the disclosure-to-exploitation timeline into a matter of hours. CERT-In's emergency directive mandating a 12-hour containment window for internet-facing crown jewels [46, 53, 112, 194] reflects this brutal new reality. The guidance explicitly acknowledges that full patching often exceeds this window, forcing defenders to rely on immediate network isolation, WAF rules, or service disablement before coordinated remediation can occur.

This acceleration is compounded by AI-driven vulnerability discovery. Anthropic's Project Glasswing scanned over 1,000 open-source projects in a single month and identified approximately 10,000 vulnerabilities, with a 90.6% true-positive rate and 62.4% validated as high or critical severity [11, 21, 47, 79]. The bottleneck is no longer finding flaws; it is human capacity to triage, patch, test, and deploy fixes. Only 75 of 530 disclosed bugs have been patched so far, leaving enterprises exposed to a widening gap between AI discovery and human remediation [47].

The operational impact is immediate and severe. Defenders can no longer rely on CVSS scores or vendor patch schedules. Internet-exposed systems are being probed and exploited autonomously the moment a vulnerability enters the public domain. Organizations must treat unpatched assets as already compromised and shift to continuous threat exposure management that prioritizes real exploitability over theoretical risk.

timeline.event: CERT-In drafts initial advisory on AI-assisted exploitation timelines
timeline.significance: Signals regulatory recognition that traditional patching SLAs are obsolete

timeline.event: CERT-In officially mandates 12-hour patching/mitigation window for internet-facing flaws
timeline.significance: Forces enterprises to implement automated containment and compensating controls

timeline.event: Anthropic confirms Project Glasswing discovered 10,000+ vulnerabilities in one month
timeline.significance: Proves AI discovery outpaces human patching capacity by orders of magnitude

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Global enterprise IT, critical infrastructure, cloud providers, and open-source maintainers face immediate exposure. Unpatched internet-facing systems are compromised within hours of disclosure.
threat_stories.action_required: Implement automated 12-hour containment playbooks for crown jewel assets. Deploy WAF/IPS rules for zero-day mitigation when patching is delayed. Shift vulnerability prioritization from CVSS to real-time exploit intelligence. Enforce network microsegmentation to limit lateral movement from compromised perimeter devices.

threat_stories.headline: Supply Chain Carnage: GitHub, npm, and the Developer Workstation Under Siege
threat_stories.narrative:
The open-source and developer tooling ecosystem is experiencing coordinated, industrialized poisoning. Attackers have moved beyond simple typosquatting to sophisticated supply chain compromises that weaponize CI/CD pipelines, Git tagging systems, and trusted package registries. The Megalodon campaign abused compromised Personal Access Tokens and deploy keys to inject base64-encoded bash payloads into GitHub Actions workflows across 5,500 public repositories [12, 58]. These payloads executed during CI/CD runs to harvest cloud credentials, SSH keys, and OIDC tokens, exfiltrating them to attacker-controlled infrastructure.

Simultaneously, attackers executed a Git tag poisoning attack against Laravel-Lang Composer packages. By rewriting over 700 historical tags to point to malicious fork commits, they ensured that developers installing or updating packages automatically executed a cross-platform PHP information stealer [35, 65]. The malware harvested AWS, Azure, and GCP credentials, Kubernetes secrets, and CI/CD tokens before exfiltrating them. The @tanstack npm packages and Nx Console VS Code extension suffered similar fate, with 84 malicious package versions and a trojanized extension v18.95.0 distributed via trusted channels [181].

This campaign dismantles the foundational trust model of modern software development. Developers are no longer just endpoints; they are the primary attack surface for enterprise infrastructure. Compromised CI/CD secrets and poisoned dependencies provide attackers with legitimate credentials and execution contexts that bypass traditional perimeter defenses.

timeline.event: Laravel-Lang Git tags are rewritten to point to malicious fork commits
timeline.significance: Establishes supply chain poisoning vector via historical tag manipulation

timeline.event: Megalodon campaign injects base64-encoded payloads into 5,500 GitHub repositories
timeline.significance: Demonstrates mass CI/CD pipeline compromise via abused PATs and workflow dispatch

timeline.event: CISA adds @tanstack and Nx Console flaws to Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
timeline.significance: Confirms active exploitation of compromised developer tooling and package registries

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Software development teams, CI/CD infrastructure, and enterprise cloud environments face severe credential exposure and persistent backdoor access. Compromised packages propagate silently through build pipelines.
threat_stories.action_required: Immediately rotate all CI/CD secrets, PATs, and deploy keys. Audit composer.lock and package.json for compromised dependencies. Enforce branch protection rules requiring pull requests for workflow changes. Implement strict IDE extension allowlists. Deploy OIDC token request monitoring to detect anomalous workflow executions.

threat_stories.headline: MFA is Dead: Real-Time Interception and OAuth Token Theft
threat_stories.narrative:
Multi-factor authentication has been systematically dismantled by live-proxy PhaaS platforms and OAuth abuse. Threat actors no longer rely on static credential dumping. Instead, they deploy real-time interception panels that proxy victim sessions, capture OTPs seconds before expiration, and authenticate simultaneously on attacker-controlled devices [7, 192]. The Chinese-language PhaaS ecosystem leverages AI-generated phishing pages and encrypted delivery channels like RCS and iMessage to bypass carrier-side filtering and signature-based detection.

Microsoft 365 environments face a parallel collapse via the Kali365 phishing kit. Attackers trick users into entering legitimate OAuth device codes on spoofed landing pages, granting them valid access and refresh tokens without ever knowing the user's password [26, 43, 78, 84]. These tokens bypass MFA entirely, allowing attackers to access Outlook, Teams, and OneDrive, register additional trusted devices, and create malicious inbox rules to suppress security alerts. Even encrypted communications are compromised; a coordinated SMS campaign impersonating Signal Support is stealing 64-character backup recovery keys to decrypt historical message archives [226, 255].

Identity verification is broken. Static passwords and SMS/OTP-based MFA provide a false sense of security. Attackers operate in the session layer, intercepting authentication flows in real-time and leveraging stolen tokens to maintain persistent, undetectable access.

timeline.event: FBI warns of Kali365 phishing kit bypassing MFA via OAuth device code abuse
timeline.significance: Highlights systemic failure of traditional MFA in enterprise environments

timeline.event: Analysis reveals PhaaS platforms intercepting OTPs in real-time for digital wallet tokenization
timeline.significance: Demonstrates live-proxy bypass techniques defeating time-based authentication

timeline.event: Targeted SMS campaign steals Signal backup recovery keys from journalists and activists
timeline.significance: Proves social engineering bypasses even end-to-end encrypted backup systems

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Enterprise identity infrastructure, financial services, and secure communications are critically exposed. Stolen OAuth tokens and intercepted OTPs grant persistent, MFA-bypassing access to sensitive data and financial assets.
threat_stories.action_required: Block or strictly restrict Microsoft OAuth device code authentication flows. Mandate FIDO2/WebAuthn passkeys for all critical accounts. Deploy session monitoring and anomaly detection for rapid OTP usage and unauthorized device registrations. Educate users on backup key phishing and enforce out-of-band verification for support requests.

threat_stories.headline: The Silent Ransom Group: Physical Access and Extortion
threat_stories.narrative:
Extortion operators are abandoning purely digital attack chains for physical infiltration. The Silent Ransom Group (SRG) deploys operatives to pose as IT support personnel, convincing employees to plug in malicious USB drives or grant remote access to corporate networks [88, 109, 125, 134, 142, 172]. This tactic completely bypasses network segmentation, zero-trust architectures, and endpoint detection systems by leveraging human trust and physical access gaps.

Once inside, operatives install keyloggers, remote access tools, and credential harvesters. They exfiltrate sensitive data using legitimate utilities like WinSCP and Rclone, transferring files to external IPs or cloud storage before demanding ransoms. Law firms, corporate headquarters, and professional service offices are prime targets due to high-value data concentration and lax physical security protocols. The FBI explicitly warns that employees are unknowingly inviting these impersonators into firms, treating them as legitimate technical support [134, 172].

This represents a fundamental shift in extortion economics. Physical access provides immediate, high-fidelity data theft without the noise of network-based exploitation. Organizations investing heavily in digital perimeter security are leaving their most valuable assets vulnerable to human manipulation and physical infiltration.

timeline.event: FBI issues flash warning on SRG operatives visiting US law firms
timeline.significance: First official recognition of coordinated physical extortion campaigns

timeline.event: Detailed reporting confirms USB drop and remote access tool deployment tactics
timeline.significance: Validates physical access as primary initial access vector for SRG

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Legal services, corporate offices, and professional firms face immediate data theft and extortion risk. Physical security gaps directly compromise digital defense architectures.
threat_stories.action_required: Disable USB ports on all publicly accessible and employee workstations. Enforce strict visitor verification and multi-factor authentication for all IT support requests. Deploy EDR rules to detect unauthorized remote access tool installations and anomalous data transfers via WinSCP/Rclone. Conduct immediate physical security audits and employee awareness training on tech support impersonation.

threat_stories.headline: Critical Enterprise Flaws Under Active Exploitation
threat_stories.narrative:
Several enterprise security and management products are experiencing unmitigated, active exploitation. FortiClient Endpoint Management Server (CVE-2026-35616) is being abused to bypass authentication and push credential-stealing malware disguised as legitimate Fortinet updates [152, 188, 189, 198]. Attackers leverage the trusted endpoint management infrastructure to deploy EKZ Infostealer across managed networks without direct user interaction.

Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN (CVE-2026-0257) allows authentication bypass, granting attackers unauthorized remote access to corporate networks [256, 267]. KnowledgeDeliver LMS installations are hit by a zero-day deserialization flaw (CVE-2026-5426) that enables unauthenticated remote code execution and Godzilla web shell deployment [8, 49, 62, 106]. Drupal Core (CVE-2026-9082) and LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin (CVE-2026-48172) face active SQLi and root privilege escalation campaigns [1, 36, 91, 102, 148].

Attackers are targeting the very tools defenders rely on for perimeter control, endpoint management, and web publishing. Compromise of these systems provides immediate network access, credential harvesting, and persistent backdoors. The exploitation is automated, widespread, and relentless.

timeline.event: CISA adds Drupal Core CVE-2026-9082 to Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
timeline.significance: Confirms active mass exploitation of web application infrastructure

timeline.event: FortiClient EMS CVE-2026-35616 actively exploited to push EKZ Infostealer
timeline.significance: Demonstrates weaponization of trusted endpoint management infrastructure

timeline.event: Palo Alto GlobalProtect CVE-2026-0257 exploitation confirmed in targeted attacks
timeline.significance: Validates authentication bypass as primary initial access vector for enterprise networks

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Enterprise networks, endpoint management systems, and web applications face immediate compromise. Exploited security tools provide attackers with legitimate credentials and execution contexts.
threat_stories.action_required: Patch FortiClient EMS to 7.4.7+, PAN-OS to latest firmware, Drupal and LiteSpeed immediately. Restrict EMS console access to dedicated management VLANs. Deploy WAF rules for known exploit patterns. Monitor for anomalous update pushes, VPN authentication bypass attempts, and unauthorized web shell deployments.

actor_profiles.name: GREYVIBE
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Leveraging AI tools (ChatGPT, Gemini, Ideogram) to generate custom malware, obfuscate payloads, and craft phishing lures targeting Ukrainian military, government, and civilian sectors. Deploying LegionRelay RAT and FallSpy Android spyware via fake CAPTCHA pages and spoofed login screens. Exhibiting OpSec failures including test sample uploads to VirusTotal and informal development artifacts.

actor_profiles.name: Silent Ransom Group (SRG)
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Deploying operatives to physically visit corporate offices and law firms, posing as IT support to convince employees to plug in malicious USB drives or grant remote access. Installing keyloggers and RATs, exfiltrating data via WinSCP/Rclone, and demanding ransoms. Bypassing digital defenses through human manipulation and physical access.

actor_profiles.name: ShinyHunters
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Conducting large-scale social engineering and credential compromise campaigns against major enterprises. Successfully breached Carnival Corporation (6M records) and Charter Communications (4.9M records) via employee deception and internal system navigation. Exfiltrating personal data and threatening public leaks or dark web sales.

actor_profiles.name: Megalodon / TrapDoor Operators
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Abusing compromised Personal Access Tokens and deploy keys to inject base64-encoded payloads into GitHub Actions workflows across 5,500 repositories. Distributing trojanized npm, PyPI, and CratesIO packages to harvest cloud credentials, SSH keys, and OIDC tokens. Targeting developer environments to gain downstream enterprise access.

actor_profiles.name: Nimbus Manticore
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Deploying AI-assisted malware (MiniJunk, MiniFast) via trojanized Zoom installers and SEO poisoning targeting fake Oracle SQL Developer downloads. Leveraging .NET AppDomain hijacking to load rogue DLLs under trusted processes and hijacking legitimate Zoom scheduled tasks for persistence. Targeting aviation and software companies.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: SQL injection vulnerability in Drupal Core API allowing unauthenticated remote attackers to inject arbitrary SQL commands on PostgreSQL-backed sites. Exploitation leads to information disclosure, privilege escalation, and remote code execution.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply the critical security patch released by Drupal on May 20, 2026. Federal agencies must remediate by May 27, 2026.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Privilege escalation vulnerability allowing arbitrary script execution as root via improper handling of Redis enable/disable functions. Any cPanel user can exploit this flaw to execute unauthorized actions.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade the LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin to version 2.4.7 or later. Review cPanel logs for suspicious Redis-related API calls.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Improper access control allowing unauthenticated attackers to bypass authentication via API endpoints. Crafted HTTP requests enable privilege escalation and remote code execution, abused to push EKZ Infostealer.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Install hotfixes for FortiClient EMS 7.4.5/7.4.6 immediately, or upgrade to version 7.4.7.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Authentication bypass vulnerability allowing remote attackers to circumvent authentication controls and establish unauthorized GlobalProtect VPN connections to internal networks.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Immediately apply the latest security patches and firmware updates provided by Palo Alto Networks for PAN-OS.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: ASP.NET ViewState deserialization vulnerability caused by hardcoded, identical machine keys across deployments. Enables unauthenticated remote code execution and web shell deployment.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Generate and deploy unique, cryptographically strong machine keys per instance. Update web.config files and apply vendor patches.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: SQL injection flaw in the Content API allowing unauthenticated attackers to read database contents, steal Admin API keys, and inject malicious JavaScript for ClickFix campaigns.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Immediately update Ghost CMS to the latest patched version. Rotate all Admin API keys and database credentials.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Supply chain compromise of 42 @tanstack npm packages containing credential-stealing malware. Attackers abused GitHub Actions and OIDC token theft from runner memory.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Audit and remove compromised npm packages. Rotate all exposed credentials and secrets. Reinstall clean versions from verified sources.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Unpatched Windows zero-day vulnerability (YellowKey) disclosed without vendor coordination. Working proof-of-concept code published publicly, rapidly weaponized by threat actors.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply Microsoft security updates immediately upon release. Monitor MSRC advisories for patches addressing YellowKey, GreenPlasma, and MiniPlasma.

hunting_leads.title: OAuth Device Code Abuse & Token Theft
hunting_leads.context: Kali365 and PhaaS platforms bypass MFA by tricking users into entering OAuth device codes, granting attackers valid refresh tokens. Hunt for anomalous device code authentication flows and rapid token issuance.
hunting_leads.query: AzureSigninLogs | where AuthenticationProtocol =~ "OAuth" and AuthenticationProcessingDetails contains "device_code" | project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, AppDisplayName, AuthenticationResult | order by TimeGenerated desc

hunting_leads.title: FortiClient EMS Malicious Update Pushes
hunting_leads.context: Attackers exploit CVE-2026-35616 to push EKZ Infostealer disguised as legitimate Fortinet endpoint updates. Hunt for anomalous PowerShell execution or configuration pushes originating from EMS consoles.
hunting_leads.query: SecurityEvent | where EventID == 4688 and ProcessCommandLine contains "powershell" and ProcessCommandLine contains "EMS" or ProcessCommandLine contains "FortiClient" | project TimeGenerated, Computer, ProcessCommandLine, SubjectUserName | order by TimeGenerated desc

hunting_leads.title: GitHub Actions Megalodon Payload Execution
hunting_leads.context: Megalodon campaign injects base64-encoded bash payloads into workflow files to exfiltrate CI/CD secrets. Hunt for unexpected workflow_dispatch executions and OIDC token requests from unknown workflows.
hunting_leads.query: GitHubAuditLog | where Action =~ "workflow_job.run" and (Payload contains "base64" or Payload contains "workflow_dispatch") | project TimeGenerated, RepoName, WorkflowName, Actor, Payload | order by TimeGenerated desc

hunting_leads.title: Signal Backup Key Phishing Indicators
hunting_leads.context: Targeted SMS campaigns impersonate Signal Support to steal 64-character backup recovery keys. Hunt for endpoint logs or DLP alerts referencing recovery key extraction or Signal support impersonation.
hunting_leads.query: EndpointLogs | where (Message contains "recovery key" or Message contains "64-character" or Message contains "Signal Support") and (EventType == "ProcessCreate" or EventType == "ClipboardAccess") | project TimeGenerated, DeviceName, ProcessName, Message | order by TimeGenerated desc

hunting_leads.title: SRG Physical Access & USB Drop Artifacts
hunting_leads.context: Silent Ransom Group operatives physically visit offices to plug in malicious USB drives or install RATs. Hunt for unauthorized USB device connections, remote access tool installations, and anomalous data transfers.
hunting_leads.query: DeviceFileEvents | where ActionType == "FileCreated" and (FolderPath contains "WinSCP" or FolderPath contains "Rclone" or FolderPath contains "AnyDesk") and InitiatingProcessFileName != "explorer.exe" | project TimeGenerated, DeviceName, FileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine | order by TimeGenerated desc

{'summary': 'Real-time MFA/OTP interception and credential harvesting via PhaaS platforms', 'severity': 'high', 'affected_products': ['SMS/OTP-based MFA systems', 'Digital Wallet Provisioning Services', 'RCS/iMessage Clients', 'Web Browsers'], 'complexity': 'low', 'cvss_score': 0.0, 'workarounds': ['Enforce FIDO2/WebAuthn security keys for critical accounts', 'Delay or limit SMS/OTP delivery frequency', 'Implement step-up verification for digital wallet provisioning', 'Use email or voice calls for out-of-band verification instead of SMS/RCS'], 'references': ['Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) Analysis: 2 PhaaS 2 Furious', 'Article: 2 PhaaS 2 Furious: The Evolution of Chinese-language Phishing Services (2026-05-25)'], 'integrity_impact': 'high', 'description': "Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) platforms enable attackers to bypass multifactor authentication (MFA) through real-time interception. When a victim enters credentials on a dynamically generated phishing page, the data is instantly relayed to an attacker's live administration panel. The attacker simultaneously triggers an OTP request on their own device, capturing the code seconds before expiration. Stolen credentials and OTPs are then used to provision payment cards into digital wallets on attacker-controlled devices, enabling tokenization for unauthorized transactions. Delivery leverages end-to-end encrypted channels like RCS and iMessage to bypass carrier security filters. AI-powered tools (e.g., Puppeteer) generate unique, high-fidelity phishing pages to evade signature-based detection, while anti-bot verification screens hinder automated security analysis.", 'attack_vector': 'network', 'privileges_required': 'none', 'exploit_maturity': 'high', 'availability_impact': 'low', 'remediation': 'Transition authentication infrastructure to phishing-resistant standards such as FIDO2/WebAuthn. Implement risk-based authentication and device fingerprinting during digital wallet provisioning. Deploy on-device security controls and link inspection for RCS/iMessage. Utilize AI-driven behavioral analytics to detect anomalous login patterns and rapid OTP usage.', 'exploit_available': 'yes', 'cve_id': 'N/A', 'cvss_vector': 'N/A', 'user_interaction': 'required', 'scope': 'changed', 'confidentiality_impact': 'high', 'published_date': '2026-05-25', 'discovery_date': ''}
statistics.key_changes: AI has compressed the vulnerability-to-exploitation timeline from weeks to hours, forcing regulatory bodies like CERT-In to mandate 12-hour containment windows for internet-facing assets. MFA bypass techniques have matured from static credential theft to real-time live-proxy interception and OAuth token harvesting. Supply chain attacks have shifted from typosquatting to industrialized poisoning of Git tags, CI/CD workflows, and trusted package registries.
emerging_trends: AI-assisted iterative LLM attacks bypassing single-turn safety benchmarks
Physical extortion campaigns leveraging tech support impersonation and USB drops
Developer ecosystem targeting via GitHub Actions abuse and Git tag rewriting
Real-time PhaaS platforms intercepting OTPs and provisioning digital wallets
Exploitation of trusted endpoint management and VPN infrastructure
declining_threats: Traditional static phishing campaigns relying on credential stuffing
Isolated ransomware deployments without prior data exfiltration
Opportunistic web defacement and low-impact DDoS attacks
Single-vector zero-day exploitation without automated scaling
